commit.message:
fix: remove dprst_area_max from output (#162) (#167)

* fix: remove dprst_area_max from output — pywatershed computes internally (#162)

dprst_area_max is not an input parameter to pywatershed. It's computed
at runtime as dprst_frac × hru_area in PRMSRunoff and PRMSSoilzone.
Remove from parameter_metadata.yml, derivation code, config model,
and tests. dprst_frac and hru_area remain in output for anyone needing
the absolute waterbody area.

Closes #162

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove stale dprst_area_max refs from docs and reference files

Update authoritative reference docs (pywatershed_dataset_param_map.yml,
parameter_inventory.md, hydroparam_dataset_parameter_map.jsx) and fix
stale docstring in _derive_waterbody(). Caught by PR review.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Rich McDonald <rich@connectedwatersllc.com>

commit.message:
Update site stats and add Day 16 blog entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement OAuth2 with SQLite database and DPAPI encryption

Major changes:
- Replace JSON config with encrypted SQLite database (config.db)
- Implement OAuth2 JWT token-based authentication (not cookies)
- Add DPAPI (Windows Data Protection API) encryption tied to user credentials
- First run generates random key, encrypts with DPAPI, stores as .dbkey (hidden)
- Subsequent runs decrypt using current Windows user credentials only
- Local HTTP listener on port 52805 receives OAuth callback token
- Bearer token sent in Authorization header for authenticated uploads
- Remove all MessageBox dialogs, use Debug.WriteLine instead
- Filter OAuth callback URL (127.0.0.1:52805) from window history
- Add build targets to copy SQLite.Interop.dll (x86/x64) to output directory
- Settings window only opens if server endpoint not configured (not based on file)
- Improved error handling and debug logging throughout

Configuration:
- ScreenShot.csproj: SQLite reference v1.0.112.1, build targets for native DLLs
- App.config: Binding redirect for System.Data.SQLite v1.0.112.1
- Constants.cs: Use AppData for config path (not DEBUG directory)
- Config.cs: DPAPI password derivation, simplified initialization
- WindowHistory.cs: Filter 127.0.0.1:52805 callback URL
- App.xaml.cs: OAuth2 flow with styled success page, config init error handling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Winback HQ date off-by-one: DATE columns parsed as UTC shifted display by one day

new Date("YYYY-MM-DD") parses date-only strings as UTC midnight, which in
US timezones (west of UTC) renders as the previous day. Fixed all DATE column
display/filter paths to use parseISO() (local time) and format() date strings
instead of .toISOString() for DATE column comparisons.

Display fixes: earliest_winback_date, calculated_winback_date,
termination_effective_date, cancel_date, pending_cancel_date,
renewal_effective_date, quote_date, sale_date across 6 components.

Filter fixes: date range queries against DATE columns now use YYYY-MM-DD
strings. Staff activity summary sends pre-computed local timezone boundaries
to edge function instead of ambiguous date string.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove text from answer buttons so Zoe must blend phonemes instead of
letter-matching. Replace native emojis with 142 Noto Color Emoji SVGs
for consistent cross-device rendering. Curate all stage 1-4 word lists
to only contain concrete, depictable nouns. Upgrade Next.js to 15.5.12
to fix 5 high-severity CVEs blocking Vercel deployment.

commit.message:
Full MS17-010 EternalBlue Implementation

Implement complete EternalBlue (CVE-2017-0144) exploit module:
- Stage 1-2: SMB1 negotiation, anonymous auth, vulnerability probe,
  non-paged pool heap grooming via NT_TRANS, SrvOs2FeaListToNt
  integer overflow trigger
- Stage 3A: Embedded x64 kernel + userland shellcode with runtime
  LHOST/LPORT patching (772-byte ring0→ring3 APC injector +
  464-byte reverse TCP shell)
- Stage 3B: SrvNet buffer corruption shellcode delivery via fake
  SRVNET_BUFFER_HDR struct targeting HAL heap (0xffffffffffd04000)
- Stage 4: Error handling for all failure paths, inline reverse shell
  listener with 15s timeout and automatic session registration,
  5 unit tests (handshake safety, shellcode patching, struct size)

* feat(test-suite): define quest with 6 missions

IntegratoR Test Suite quest with enriched mission specs:
- testkit: shared test infrastructure (TestKit Foundation)
- abstractions: domain primitives and Result types (~51 tests)
- application: pipeline behaviours, CQRS handlers (~92 tests)
- odata: generic OData client and infrastructure (~59 tests)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(test-suite): clean up Application Tests

Mark application mission as completed in index. Update agent memory
with MediatR delegate signature, NSubstitute visibility, and
FluentValidation open-generic registration pitfalls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(odata): migrate from Simple.OData.Client to PanoramicData.OData.Client

Replace unmaintained Simple.OData.Client 6.0.1 with PanoramicData.OData.Client
10.0.55, an actively maintained successor targeting .NET 10 with minimal
dependencies.

Key changes:
- Introduce IODataClientAdapter wrapper for testability (PanoramicData's
  ODataClient is concrete with no interface)
- Rewrite ODataExceptionHandler to use PanoramicData's typed exception
  hierarchy (ODataNotFoundException, ODataUnauthorizedException, etc.)
- Rewrite ODataService method bodies to use adapter pattern
- Update DI registration: ODataClientOptions replaces ODataClientSettings
- Remove AppContext.SetSwitch("Switch.System.Xml.AllowDefaultResolver")
  security concern
- Enable CentralPackageTransitivePinningEnabled for dependency hygiene
- Remove Microsoft.OData.Core and Microsoft.OData.Edm transitive deps
- Update all 303 tests (0 failures)
- Document PanoramicData-specific features for future adoption

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(odata): address review findings from PR #38

- Cache JsonSerializerOptions as static readonly field in ODataClientAdapter
- Extract duplicated Result.Fail<T> reflection into shared CreateFailResult
  helper with ConcurrentDictionary<Type, MethodInfo> cache
- Remove redundant LogSuccess(TimeSpan.Zero) from ExecuteCollectionAsync
- Materialize IEnumerable in batch methods to prevent double enumeration
- Add warning log on key property count mismatch in BuildCompositeKeyObject
- Cache [Key] attribute reflection in static KeyPropertyCache
- Replace null! AsyncRetryPolicy singleton with proper no-op policy
- Simplify overlapping switch arms in IsTransientError

Fix webcam double-logging, lightstation date bug, and analytics pipeline

- fetch_webcam.py: disable console logging (cron redirects stdout to log
  file, causing every line to appear twice)
- parse_lightstation.py: handle day-out-of-range when rolling back month
  (e.g. day=31 rolled to February); try one additional month before giving up

commit.message:
Add city landing pages, fix location-specific sightings, and improve SEO

Create 201 city landing pages targeting "birding near [city]" queries with
low search competition. Each page includes nearby locations with distance,
species/habitat/season breakdowns, FAQ schema, and internal linking.

Fix notable sightings to use eBird's geo-based API instead of state-level
region codes, so each location shows its own nearby sightings.

Wire up OpenGraph, Twitter cards, canonical URLs, and breadcrumb JSON-LD
on state and location pages using the existing createMetadata() utility.

feat: recently viewed skills + better Ask suggestion chips (#242)

Adds a "Recently Viewed" strip to the Ask modal empty state (localStorage,
no backend, no auth). When a user visits a skill detail page the skill is
saved to localStorage (capped at 5, deduplicated). On next modal open the
strip appears above the suggestion chips with skill name, grade badge, and
description.

Also replaces the three generic suggestion chips with more specific,
prose-style queries that model real-world use patterns.

commit.message:
Add in-memory TTL cache for hot read paths (#233)

* feat: add application-level caching for hot read paths

Add a two-layer caching strategy for frequently-read, slowly-changing
endpoints: HTTP Cache-Control headers (for browsers/CDN) and an
in-memory TTL cache (per Modal container, avoids repeated DB queries).

Cached endpoints:
- /v1/taxonomy (300s) — static data, Cache-Control only
- /v1/stats (60s) — in-memory + Cache-Control
- /v1/orgs/profiles (60s) — in-memory + Cache-Control
- /v1/orgs/stats (60s) — in-memory keyed by query params + Cache-Control
- /v1/orgs/{slug}/profile (60s) — Cache-Control only
- /v1/skills (30s, anonymous only) — in-memory keyed by params + Cache-Control
- /sitemap.xml (300s) — in-memory + Cache-Control

All TTLs are configurable via Settings (cache_ttl_* fields). Authenticated
skill list requests bypass caching entirely since visibility varies per user.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Cjs7ZmbmXMf28g5KacPi2n

* fix: compute cache_key once to prevent silent inconsistency

Move cache_key computation above the conditional blocks so it's defined
once and reused for both the get and set operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove duplicate user message in LLM context

build_context() appended the user message explicitly AFTER it was
already in _message_cache (added by save_message). This caused the
LLM to see every user message twice — explaining "duplicate message"
perception without actual Telegram or log duplicates.

Fix: remove the explicit append. Media metadata (image/audio/doc)
is now attached to the cached message entry for the current turn.

v0.28.3

commit.message:
perf(reporter-ui): memoize TrendChart data and stabilize Recharts prop references (#113)

* perf(reporter-ui): memoize TrendChart data and stabilize Recharts prop references

- Wrap chartData computation in useMemo to avoid recreating the array on
  every render; dependency is [historical]
- Extract CHART_MARGIN, AXIS_TICK_STYLE, LINE_DOT_STYLE, LINE_ACTIVE_DOT_STYLE
  to module-level constants so Recharts never sees new object references
- Extract formatPercent to a module-level function to stabilize the
  tickFormatter prop reference
- Change <Tooltip content={<CustomTooltip />} /> to content={CustomTooltip}
  so Recharts receives the component class rather than a new element each render;
  update CustomTooltip signature to accept TooltipContentProps from recharts
- Define PASS_COLOR / MUTED_COLOR named constants with a comment explaining
  why hex literals are required (Recharts SVG props cannot resolve CSS variables)
- Rename chartData fields from {date, passRate} to {label, value, timestamp}
  per CHK-011 spec; retain passed/total for tooltip display
- Add role="img" and aria-label with trend summary to outer wrapper (CHK bonus)
